generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ANTIMONIUM ARSENITUM. Arsenite of antimony. Formula , (Sb 2 O 3 ) AsO 5 + 3 p. c. (For chemical preparation, see All. Hom. Zeit., 79, 76) Preparation , Triturations. Authority. Isnard (A. H. Z., 79, 76, Review of Therap. Andw. des Arsen.) took daily increasing doses of .01 to 0.10 of centigramme; symptoms began on the sixth day. ► Congestion of the head. ► Pain in the forehead. ► Pressure in the temples. ► Pain in the orbits. ► Conjunctivitis. ► dema of the face. ► General sick feeling. ► Sense of weakness. ► Wandering pains along the sciatic nerves. ► Loss of appetite. ► Nausea. ► Diarrha slight, without pain in abdomen. ► Pulse 90. ► (*Excessive dyspna in cases of emphysema) , Dr. Payr, A. H. Z., 79. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
General sick feeling. Sense of weakness.

head.text: Rush of blood to head. Pain in forehead. Pressure in temples.

eyes.text: Pain in orbits. Inflammation of eyes.

stomach_abdomen.text: APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Ant. ar.]. Loss of appetite. Nausea.

stool_rectum.text: Slight diarrhœa, without pain in abdomen.
